data.title: Renderer pool to lift the 1-render-at-a-time ceiling

data.body:
Severity: performance high. Source: TYB-12-followup performance audit.

Where: server/internal/render/render.go:35-115. The Renderer struct holds a single *resvg.Renderer behind a single sync.Mutex; every RenderPNG call holds the mutex start-to-finish.

What: resvg-go wasm is non-concurrent; the project today has exactly one instance. Renders are 0.5-2s on x86_64 wasm for 3600x4800 PNGs. The render path is on the critical path of /api/render AND /api/checkout/start, so the entire site is capped at ~1 req/s aggregate.

Impact: any concurrent buyer queues behind the renderer mutex; under 50 unique designs in a burst (singleflight only collapses identical hashes), tail latency = 50× single-render time. The 60s WriteTimeout is the only thing keeping the queue from growing unbounded; once a request times out mid-render, the wasm work still completes (no ctx propagation) — wasted cycles.

Recommendation: build a pool of N *resvg.Renderer instances behind a buffered semaphore channel. Configurable via RENDERER_POOL_SIZE env var; default to GOMAXPROCS or 4. The package comment at render.go:35-36 already pre-approves this direction. Effort: small (50-100 LoC). Even N=4 raises the ceiling 4×.

Notes for planning: resvg-go v0.0.1's Close() is documented as broken — the pool design should share the *resvg.Context but allocate per-instance Renderers; investigate carefully. Pairs naturally with TYB-20 (per-call timeout).
